schema.appDescription: Calculate monthly and annual interest earned on savings account balance. Compare savings account vs FD vs liquid fund returns.

inputs.label: Interest Rate (% p.a.)
inputs.hint: SBI/HDFC: 3.5%; Small finance banks: up to 7%

howToSteps.name: Enter your details
howToSteps.text: Fill in the input fields for the Savings Account Interest Calculator.

howToSteps.name: View instant results
howToSteps.text: Results update in real time as you type.

howToSteps.name: Explore breakdown
howToSteps.text: Open the year-by-year breakdown to see numbers over time.

howToSteps.name: Compare scenarios
howToSteps.text: Adjust inputs to compare scenarios and optimise your decision.

faq.q: What is the savings account interest rate in India 2026?
faq.a: Major banks: SBI 3.0%, HDFC Bank 3.5%, ICICI Bank 3.5%, Axis Bank 3.5%. Small finance banks: AU Small Finance 7%, Unity Small Finance 6%, Equitas 5.5%. Interest is calculated daily on closing balance and credited quarterly. TDS applies if interest exceeds ₹10,000/year.

faq.q: How is savings account interest calculated?
faq.a: Interest = Daily Closing Balance × Annual Rate ÷ 365. Interest is calculated daily and credited quarterly (Jan, Apr, Jul, Oct). You earn interest on the daily balance, so withdrawals reduce interest. Maintain higher balance on month-end for better interest calculations in some banks.

faq.q: Should I keep large amounts in savings account?
faq.a: No. Savings accounts pay 3.5–7% while FDs offer 7–8% and liquid funds 7–8.5% with daily liquidity. For idle money above ₹1 lakh: sweep-in FD (automatically sweeps to FD at threshold), liquid funds (redeemable T+1, similar returns to savings for high balances), or ultra-short-term debt funds.
No. Calculations run in your browser.
Yes. Works on all modern smartphones.

examples.heading: ₹5 lakh in SBI savings account for 12 months
examples.text: ₹5 lakh at 3.0% for 12 months: monthly interest ₹1,250, annual ₹15,000. Compared with SBI FD at 6.8%: annual interest ₹34,000. Keeping ₹5 lakh in savings instead of FD costs ₹19,000/year in forgone interest.

examples.heading: AU Small Finance Bank — 7% savings account
examples.text: ₹5 lakh at 7% (AU Small Finance): annual interest ₹35,000. Nearly same as FD but with instant liquidity. However, deposits are insured only up to ₹5 lakh per bank under DICGC — important if balance approaches this limit.
